title: Bot AI Fixes: Infinite Loop & Starvation Timer
content:
## Overview

Two related problems were fixed together in commit `4e91f64c`:

1. **Infinite loop / oscillation** — bots in Iron Snake mode (and occasionally classic mode) would get permanently stuck in approach-and-retreat cycles, never making progress.
2. **Starvation** — even after the loop guard was added, a bot that wanders productively but never reaches food could keep the game running indefinitely with no automatic resolution.

---

## Problem 1: Bot Oscillation Cycles

### Root Cause

Every bot (`src/bots/*.ts`) is a **stateless, per-tick, greedy direction-scorer** that returns only a `Direction`. Given an identical board configuration, the scoring produces an identical choice. When a bot paths toward food, correctly refuses to enter a tight pocket (it would trap itself), and retreats, the deterministic scoring re-selects the exact same losing approach from the retreated position — creating a stable, never-breaking cycle. Iron Snake mode's irregular board masks create far more tight pockets, which is why the problem surfaces there most often. This is inherent to the architecture, not a bug in any individual bot.

### Solution: Centralized Loop Guard (`src/bots/loop-guard.ts`)

Rather than adding memory to all 11 stateless bots (which would require changing the shared bot interface and rewriting every bot), a **harness-level loop guard** was added. It intercepts the chosen direction only when a loop is actually detected, leaving all bot personalities completely intact during normal play.

#### Detection: Two Signals

A single revisit counter proved insufficient. A pure revisit counter (3 hits in a window) cannot catch a ~20-cell "approach, give up, wander back" circuit — on a period-20 loop, no cell recurs 3 times until 60 moves have passed. Two signals are used together:

- **Tight oscillation:** any cell revisited **3+ times in a 40-move rolling window** of recent head positions.
- **Confinement ratio:** too few distinct cells relative to total moves over a 24-move sample — catches larger circuits that the revisit counter alone misses. Confirmed by simulation: the guard broke a bot out of a 20-cell ring to explore 125 cells.

#### Escape Behavior

When a loop is detected, `chooseEscapeDirection` picks the legal, non-trapping move whose destination cell has been **visited least recently**, with random tie-breaking. The snake commits to this escape direction for **8 ticks** (`ESCAPE_DURATION`) to prevent it from snapping back immediately. The escape direction still passes the existing `isValidDirection` + `simulateMove` collision re-validation, so no illegal move is possible.

#### Exports

- `type LoopMemory = { recentHeads: string[]; escapeTicks: number }`
- `createLoopMemory(): LoopMemory`
- `resetLoopMemory(mem)` — clears history and escape counter
- `recordHead(mem, head: Position)` — pushes `"x,y"` into the capped ring buffer
- `isLooping(mem): boolean`
- `chooseEscapeDirection(state, helpers, mem): Direction | null` — returns `null` if no safe move exists (harness keeps the bot's original pick)

All logic uses the existing `BotHelpers` (`simulateMove`, `analyzePosition`, `getCandidateDirections`), so it automatically respects the Iron Snake `boardMask`.

### Harness Wiring (`src/main.ts`)

- Per-snake `LoopMemory` fields: `botLoopMemory` (P1/single-player) and `botLoopMemory2` (P2), initialized with `createLoopMemory()`.
- In `chooseBotDirection` and `chooseBotDirectionForPlayer`: after the bot picks its direction, `recordHead` is called; if `escapeTicks > 0` or `isLooping` is true, `chooseEscapeDirection` is called and its result replaces the bot's pick.
- `resetLoopMemory` is called at every progress point: food eaten (single-player and two-player), `advanceLevel`, `repositionSnakes`, and all game start/restart paths.

### Menu Demo (`src/menu-demo.ts`)

The title-screen background animation has its own bot harness. The same `loop-guard` module is wired there (one `LoopMemory` per demo snake) to eliminate oscillation from the menu visuals, reusing the shared module without duplication.

---

## Problem 2: Starvation Timer

### Solution: Move Budget with Hard Backstop

Per-snake move counters (`movesSinceFood1`, `movesSinceFood2`) in `src/main.ts` increment each tick and reset to zero whenever that snake eats. A `starvationLimit()` helper computes the budget as:

```
max(100, 2 × boardArea)
```

The limit scales with the **playable board area**, not snake length. A bigger board means food can legitimately be farther away. A competent snake never approaches the limit; it only fires as a backstop when food is genuinely unreachable. A latent bug was also fixed: `expandGrid()` doubled `gridSize` without refreshing `boardArea`, which would have made the starvation limit stale after a classic-mode grid expansion.

### Game-Over Integration

Starvation is routed through the existing `endGame` / `endGameTwoPlayer` calls already used for collision deaths, with a `deathCause` flag to adjust displayed text. This kept win/loss/draw resolution logic untouched. Two-player starvation draws (both snakes starving on the same tick) are handled for free by the existing symmetry in `endGameTwoPlayer`.

Game-over text variants:
- Single-player: **"Starved!"**
- Two-player: **"Player 2 Wins! (P1 starved)"**, **"Draw — both starved!"**, etc.

### Visual Hunger Warning

Once a snake passes **60% of its move budget** without eating, its colours blend toward an amber/red warning palette via a `lerpHex()` colour interpolator in `draw()`, ramping to full warning colour at the limit.

A **pulsing overlay banner** (`#starvation-warning` in `index.html`, styled in `src/style.css`) appears the instant the hunger tint begins and names which snake is at risk in two-player modes. It is positioned over the canvas via a `.canvas-wrap` wrapper so it does not shift the game layout. A plain urgent message was chosen over a live countdown to avoid per-tick number flicker.

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/bots/loop-guard.ts` | **New.** Shared loop-detection and escape logic. |
| `src/main.ts` | Loop guard wiring (both bot harnesses); starvation counters, limit helper, `deathCause` flag, `expandGrid` fix. |
| `src/menu-demo.ts` | Loop guard wiring for demo snakes. |
| `index.html` | `#starvation-warning` overlay element; `.canvas-wrap` wrapper. |
| `src/style.css` | Pulsing pill styles for starvation warning banner. |
